perturbation.original_text: 10.4 Payment of Invoices Each invoice provided by Supplier to Customer under Section 10.1, to the extent accurate, shall be paid by Customer to Supplier within [* * *] after the date of the invoice to the extent that Customer does not reasonably dispute that portion of the invoice in good faith. All payments will be made in U.S. Dollars by SWIFT bank transfer directly to the Supplier account as specified in the respective Purchase Orders.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.4 Payment of Invoices Each invoice provided by Supplier to Customer under Section 10.1, to the extent accurate, shall be paid by Customer to Supplier within [* * *] after the date of the invoice. However, if the Customer engages in early payment, remitting payment within 15 days, they are entitled to a 2% discount. All payments will be made in U.S. Dollars by SWIFT bank transfer directly to the Supplier account as specified in the respective Purchase Orders.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a discount for early payments (within 15 days), despite the initial statement requiring payment within [* * *]. This directly contradicts the original payment term, creating uncertainty about the actual deadline and applicable discounts. If the original timeframe (e.g. 30 days) is still the deadline, whether the supplier can expect the full payment after 15 days, creating an in-text contradiction regarding payment deadlines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 Payment of Invoices Each invoice provided by Supplier to Customer under Section 10.1, to the extent accurate, shall be paid by Customer to Supplier within [* * *] after the date of the invoice. However, if the Customer engages in early payment, remitting payment within 15 days, they are entitled to a 2% discount. All payments will be made in U.S. Dollars by SWIFT bank transfer directly to the Supplier account as specified in the respective Purchase Orders.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 2% discount for early payment within 15 days, contradicting the original payment terms that require payment within a specified timeframe ('[* * *]' after the invoice date). This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict, as the supplier may expect full payment after 15 days based on the original term, but the customer could take a discount. The contradiction is explicit and creates ambiguity about the actual payment deadline and applicable amounts, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Invoice Contents All invoices issued by Supplier under Section 10.1 shall show: a) the actual quantity of Product shipped; b) the lot number of each batch of Product shipped; c) the Fees for the quantity of Product shipped, based on the Fees for the Product set out in the applicable Purchase Order; and d) the Purchase Order number placed by Customer for the Product shipped. If Customer disputes for any reason with the amount of any invoice submitted by Supplier, Customer shall notify Supplier of such dispute within [* * *] after the date of the invoice, and the Parties shall promptly attempt to resolve the dispute. If Customer does not notify Supplier of any such dispute within such [* * *] period, such invoice will be final and binding on Customer and Supplier, subject to the correction of mathematical errors.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Invoice Contents All invoices issued by Supplier under Section 10.1 shall show: a) the actual quantity of Product shipped; b) the lot number of each batch of Product shipped; c) the Fees for the quantity of Product shipped, based on the Fees for the Product set out in the applicable Purchase Order; and d) the Purchase Order number placed by Customer for the Product shipped. If Customer disputes for any reason with the amount of any invoice submitted by Supplier, Customer shall notify Supplier of such dispute within [* * *] after the date of the invoice. However, for invoices exceeding $100,000, the dispute notification period is extended to 60 days. The Parties shall promptly attempt to resolve the dispute. If Customer does not notify Supplier of any such dispute within the initially stated period for invoices under $100,000 or the extended period for invoices over $100,000, such invoice will be final and binding on Customer and Supplier, subject to the correction of mathematical errors.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by introducing a different dispute notification period (60 days) for invoices exceeding $100,000, while the initial statement sets a single dispute period ([* * *]) for all invoices. This introduces confusion and potential legal uncertainty as to which dispute period applies based on invoice value, contradicting the initial general rule.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Invoice Contents All invoices issued by Supplier under Section 10.1 shall show: a) the actual quantity of Product shipped; b) the lot number of each batch of Product shipped; c) the Fees for the quantity of Product shipped, based on the Fees for the Product set out in the applicable Purchase Order; and d) the Purchase Order number placed by Customer for the Product shipped. If Customer disputes for any reason with the amount of any invoice submitted by Supplier, Customer shall notify Supplier of such dispute within [* * *] after the date of the invoice. However, for invoices exceeding $100,000, the dispute notification period is extended to 60 days. The Parties shall promptly attempt to resolve the dispute. If Customer does not notify Supplier of any such dispute within the initially stated period for invoices under $100,000 or the extended period for invoices over $100,000, such invoice will be final and binding on Customer and Supplier, subject to the correction of mathematical errors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new condition where invoices exceeding $100,000 have a 60-day dispute period, while the original text and the beginning of the changed text specify a single, unspecified dispute period ([* * *]) for all invoices. This directly contradicts the initial statement, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over the applicable dispute period depending on the invoice amount. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections provide conflicting deadlines, creating a contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced. The contradicted text is the same section of text where the change happened, but the introduction of a second deadline for invoices over a specified amount directly contradicts the first deadline for all invoices.

perturbation.original_text: Article 9 Fees 9.1 Fees a) Customer shall pay to Supplier, in respect of each Purchase Order placed by Customer, the applicable Fees for the supply of the Product in bulk quantities under this Agreement, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. b) Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, the Fees specified in each Purchase Order accepted by Supplier shall be full compensation for all Manufacturing and characterization activities and Materials in respect thereof. Customer shall make all requests for processing changes to be performed under this Agreement in writing under Section 3.5 and Supplier shall provide Customer a cost estimate for such work.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 9 Fees 9.1 Fees a) Customer shall pay to Supplier, in respect of each Purchase Order placed by Customer, the applicable Fees for the supply of the Product in bulk quantities under this Agreement, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. b) Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, the Fees specified in each Purchase Order accepted by Supplier shall be full compensation for all Manufacturing and characterization activities and Materials in respect thereof. However, Customer is responsible for covering 50% of any unforseen increased costs in materials, or any regulatory impact changes which affect the process, post-agreement signing, subject to agreement on the estimated costs. Customer shall make all requests for processing changes to be performed under this Agreement in writing under Section 3.5 and Supplier shall provide Customer a cost estimate for such work.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreed upon fees are the full compensation, which would mean the Supplier is responsible for any unforseen costs. By adding the clause regarding Customer's responsibility for 50% of unforseen costs in material or regulatory changes, it creates an in-text contradiction with the established agreement on full compensation, causing potential disputes on how fees cover any unforeseen costs and who has the responsibility for them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 9 Fees 9.1 Fees a) Customer shall pay to Supplier, in respect of each Purchase Order placed by Customer, the applicable Fees for the supply of the Product in bulk quantities under this Agreement, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. b) Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, the Fees specified in each Purchase Order accepted by Supplier shall be full compensation for all Manufacturing and characterization activities and Materials in respect thereof. However, Customer is responsible for covering 50% of any unforseen increased costs in materials, or any regulatory impact changes which affect the process, post-agreement signing, subject to agreement on the estimated costs. Customer shall make all requests for processing changes to be performed under this Agreement in writing under Section 3.5 and Supplier shall provide Customer a cost estimate for such work.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that the fees specified in each Purchase Order are 'full compensation' for all manufacturing, characterization activities, and materials. The changed text introduces a new clause stating that the Customer is responsible for covering 50% of unforeseen costs, thereby contradicting the initial agreement on full compensation. This creates a direct conflict within Article 9, making it unclear which terms are enforceable and leading to potential disputes, as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
